item.title: Her Husband Plotted to End Her Life With Poison But She Survived!
item.description: This video by Kendall Rae details the true crime survival story of Hannah Pettey, who was systematically poisoned with lead by her husband, Brian Man, a chiropractor from Decatur, Alabama. Under the guise of health supplements, Brian slowly poisoned Hannah while isolating her and creating a financial motive through secret life insurance policies. Following her near-fatal decline and eventual medical diagnosis, a subsequent investigation and trial led to Brian's conviction for attempted murder in 2025. The case highlights both the chilling reality of this domestic betrayal and Hannah's remarkable resilience in her recovery.

media:content.media:title: Her Husband Plotted to End Her Life With Poison But She Survived!
media:content.media:description: This video by Kendall Rae details the true crime survival story of Hannah Pettey, who was systematically poisoned with lead by her husband, Brian Man, a chiropractor from Decatur, Alabama. Under the guise of health supplements, Brian slowly poisoned Hannah while isolating her and creating a financial motive through secret life insurance policies. Following her near-fatal decline and eventual medical diagnosis, a subsequent investigation and trial led to Brian's conviction for attempted murder in 2025. The case highlights both the chilling reality of this domestic betrayal and Hannah's remarkable resilience in her recovery.

item.title: Why Did Ibraheem Yazeed Murder Aniah Blanchard?
item.description:
In October 2019, 19 year old Aniah Blanchard disappeared after stopping at a gas station in Auburn, Alabama. Investigators later determined she had been abducted and fatally shot. Her body was discovered weeks later in a wooded area, leading authorities to charge Ibraheem Yazeed with her murder.
One of the biggest questions in the case has always been motive. Prosecutors established that Yazeed kidnapped and killed Blanchard, but no clear personal connection between the two was ever uncovered. Evidence presented by investigators suggested the encounter was random rather than targeted. Surveillance footage showed the two at the same gas station, and a witness reported seeing Yazeed force Aniah into her vehicle.
Authorities have never publicly provided a definitive explanation for why Yazeed chose Aniah or what his exact motive was. As a result, the reason behind the crime remains one of the most disturbing unanswered aspects of the case. In 2026, Yazeed was convicted of murder and felony murder and later sentenced to life in prison without parole.

media:content.media:title: Why Did Ibraheem Yazeed Murder Aniah Blanchard?
media:content.media:description:
In October 2019, 19 year old Aniah Blanchard disappeared after stopping at a gas station in Auburn, Alabama. Investigators later determined she had been abducted and fatally shot. Her body was discovered weeks later in a wooded area, leading authorities to charge Ibraheem Yazeed with her murder.
One of the biggest questions in the case has always been motive. Prosecutors established that Yazeed kidnapped and killed Blanchard, but no clear personal connection between the two was ever uncovered. Evidence presented by investigators suggested the encounter was random rather than targeted. Surveillance footage showed the two at the same gas station, and a witness reported seeing Yazeed force Aniah into her vehicle.
Authorities have never publicly provided a definitive explanation for why Yazeed chose Aniah or what his exact motive was. As a result, the reason behind the crime remains one of the most disturbing unanswered aspects of the case. In 2026, Yazeed was convicted of murder and felony murder and later sentenced to life in prison without parole.

item.title: Her Poison Expert Husband Allegedly Did What?!
item.description: This video covers the tragic case of Betty Bowman, a pharmacist at the Mayo Clinic who was poisoned and killed by her husband, Connor Bowman.

media:content.media:title: Her Poison Expert Husband Allegedly Did What?!
media:content.media:description: This video covers the tragic case of Betty Bowman, a pharmacist at the Mayo Clinic who was poisoned and killed by her husband, Connor Bowman.

item.title: She Walked Into the Desert at Dawn and Was Never Seen Again
item.description:
In October 2018, 16 year old Karlie Gusé vanished from her family’s home in Chalfant, California after a frightening night in which loved ones said she appeared confused, fearful, and disoriented. By sunrise, Karlie had left the house without her phone, money, or personal belongings and was seen walking alone near Highway 6.
Multiple witnesses reported seeing Karlie that morning, but despite extensive searches involving helicopters, dogs, and law enforcement agencies, no confirmed trace of her has ever been found. Investigators have explored numerous possibilities over the years, yet no theory has fully explained how she seemingly vanished without a clue.
More than seven years later, Karlie's disappearance remains one of California's most haunting unsolved mysteries. The FBI and Mono County Sheriff's Office continue to seek information about what happened during the final hours before she disappeared.

media:content.media:title: She Walked Into the Desert at Dawn and Was Never Seen Again
media:content.media:description:
In October 2018, 16 year old Karlie Gusé vanished from her family’s home in Chalfant, California after a frightening night in which loved ones said she appeared confused, fearful, and disoriented. By sunrise, Karlie had left the house without her phone, money, or personal belongings and was seen walking alone near Highway 6.
Multiple witnesses reported seeing Karlie that morning, but despite extensive searches involving helicopters, dogs, and law enforcement agencies, no confirmed trace of her has ever been found. Investigators have explored numerous possibilities over the years, yet no theory has fully explained how she seemingly vanished without a clue.
More than seven years later, Karlie's disappearance remains one of California's most haunting unsolved mysteries. The FBI and Mono County Sheriff's Office continue to seek information about what happened during the final hours before she disappeared.

item.title: The Only Unsolved Skyjacking In American History
item.description:
On November 24, 1971, a man using the name D.B. Cooper boarded a commercial flight, claimed he had a bomb, and demanded $200,000 in ransom before parachuting from the aircraft somewhere over the Pacific Northwest. Despite one of the largest manhunts in FBI history, Cooper was never found.
Investigators spent decades pursuing leads, interviewing suspects, and analyzing physical evidence, but no theory has ever definitively explained who Cooper was or whether he survived the jump. In 1980, a portion of the ransom money was discovered along the Columbia River, only deepening the mystery.
The case remains the only unsolved skyjacking in American history and continues to fascinate investigators, journalists, and true crime enthusiasts more than 50 years later. To this day, the true identity and fate of D.B. Cooper remain unknown.

media:content.media:title: The Only Unsolved Skyjacking In American History
media:content.media:description:
On November 24, 1971, a man using the name D.B. Cooper boarded a commercial flight, claimed he had a bomb, and demanded $200,000 in ransom before parachuting from the aircraft somewhere over the Pacific Northwest. Despite one of the largest manhunts in FBI history, Cooper was never found.
Investigators spent decades pursuing leads, interviewing suspects, and analyzing physical evidence, but no theory has ever definitively explained who Cooper was or whether he survived the jump. In 1980, a portion of the ransom money was discovered along the Columbia River, only deepening the mystery.
The case remains the only unsolved skyjacking in American history and continues to fascinate investigators, journalists, and true crime enthusiasts more than 50 years later. To this day, the true identity and fate of D.B. Cooper remain unknown.

item.title: The Incredible Survival Of Nicki Lenway
item.description:
In 2022, Minneapolis crime scene investigator Nicki Lenway was ambushed while picking up her son from a parenting center. A masked attacker ran up behind her and shot her at point blank range before leaving her for dead in a parking lot. Despite suffering devastating injuries, Nicki survived the attack.
Investigators quickly discovered the shooting was far from random. Surveillance footage, digital evidence, and phone records helped uncover a murder for hire plot tied to a bitter custody dispute involving Nicki’s ex boyfriend, Tim Amacher, and his girlfriend, Colleen Larson.
The case gained national attention because of Nicki’s remarkable survival, the extensive digital trail that exposed the conspiracy, and the shocking lengths the perpetrators allegedly went to in an effort to silence her forever.

media:content.media:title: The Incredible Survival Of Nicki Lenway
media:content.media:description:
In 2022, Minneapolis crime scene investigator Nicki Lenway was ambushed while picking up her son from a parenting center. A masked attacker ran up behind her and shot her at point blank range before leaving her for dead in a parking lot. Despite suffering devastating injuries, Nicki survived the attack.
Investigators quickly discovered the shooting was far from random. Surveillance footage, digital evidence, and phone records helped uncover a murder for hire plot tied to a bitter custody dispute involving Nicki’s ex boyfriend, Tim Amacher, and his girlfriend, Colleen Larson.
The case gained national attention because of Nicki’s remarkable survival, the extensive digital trail that exposed the conspiracy, and the shocking lengths the perpetrators allegedly went to in an effort to silence her forever.

item.title: The Disappearance of Leeanna Warner
item.description:
In 2003, 5 year old Leeanna Warner vanished while walking home from a friend’s house in Chisholm, Minnesota. The short walk should have taken only minutes, but Leeanna never made it back, triggering an immediate search across the small community.
Investigators spent years following leads, interviewing potential suspects, and examining possible sightings, but no confirmed trace of Leeanna has ever been found. Authorities have long believed foul play was involved, though the case remains officially unsolved.
Leeanna’s disappearance continues to haunt Minnesota decades later, with her family and investigators still searching for answers about what happened during the brief walk home that changed everything.

media:content.media:title: The Disappearance of Leeanna Warner
media:content.media:description:
In 2003, 5 year old Leeanna Warner vanished while walking home from a friend’s house in Chisholm, Minnesota. The short walk should have taken only minutes, but Leeanna never made it back, triggering an immediate search across the small community.
Investigators spent years following leads, interviewing potential suspects, and examining possible sightings, but no confirmed trace of Leeanna has ever been found. Authorities have long believed foul play was involved, though the case remains officially unsolved.
Leeanna’s disappearance continues to haunt Minnesota decades later, with her family and investigators still searching for answers about what happened during the brief walk home that changed everything.

item.title: Family Demands Answers In Jessica Easterly's Case
item.description:
In 2009, 43 year old Jessica Easterly disappeared from New Orleans after leaving home with her estranged husband, Thomas “Tommy” Stewart. Days later, her burned vehicle was discovered abandoned, sparking a missing persons investigation that quickly raised suspicions among family members and investigators.
For months, Jessica’s loved ones publicly pushed for answers, insisting her disappearance was suspicious and criticizing the lack of progress in the case. In 2011, investigators discovered Jessica’s remains in a wooded area near the Louisiana Mississippi border, turning the investigation into a homicide case.
Authorities later arrested Stewart, who was eventually convicted in connection with Jessica’s death. The case gained widespread attention because of the disturbing details, the family’s relentless fight for justice, and the years it took before answers finally emerged.

media:content.media:title: Family Demands Answers In Jessica Easterly's Case
media:content.media:description:
In 2009, 43 year old Jessica Easterly disappeared from New Orleans after leaving home with her estranged husband, Thomas “Tommy” Stewart. Days later, her burned vehicle was discovered abandoned, sparking a missing persons investigation that quickly raised suspicions among family members and investigators.
For months, Jessica’s loved ones publicly pushed for answers, insisting her disappearance was suspicious and criticizing the lack of progress in the case. In 2011, investigators discovered Jessica’s remains in a wooded area near the Louisiana Mississippi border, turning the investigation into a homicide case.
Authorities later arrested Stewart, who was eventually convicted in connection with Jessica’s death. The case gained widespread attention because of the disturbing details, the family’s relentless fight for justice, and the years it took before answers finally emerged.

item.title: Michele MacNeill's 'Perfect Marriage' Ends In Tragedy
item.description:
Michele MacNeill appeared to have a picture perfect life in Utah with her husband, Dr. Martin MacNeill, and their eight children. But in 2007, Michele was found dead in her bathtub just days after cosmetic surgery, and authorities initially ruled her death natural causes.
Over time, Michele’s children began questioning the circumstances surrounding her death, pointing to suspicious behavior, medication concerns, and Martin MacNeill’s alleged affair. Prosecutors later argued he used Michele’s surgery and recovery as cover for what they called an “almost perfect murder.”
Martin MacNeill was eventually convicted of murdering his wife and obstructing justice. The case gained national attention because of the family’s outward image, the years of deception uncovered during the investigation, and the determination of Michele’s daughters to reopen the case after authorities initially believed no crime had occurred.

media:content.media:title: Michele MacNeill's 'Perfect Marriage' Ends In Tragedy
media:content.media:description:
Michele MacNeill appeared to have a picture perfect life in Utah with her husband, Dr. Martin MacNeill, and their eight children. But in 2007, Michele was found dead in her bathtub just days after cosmetic surgery, and authorities initially ruled her death natural causes.
Over time, Michele’s children began questioning the circumstances surrounding her death, pointing to suspicious behavior, medication concerns, and Martin MacNeill’s alleged affair. Prosecutors later argued he used Michele’s surgery and recovery as cover for what they called an “almost perfect murder.”
Martin MacNeill was eventually convicted of murdering his wife and obstructing justice. The case gained national attention because of the family’s outward image, the years of deception uncovered during the investigation, and the determination of Michele’s daughters to reopen the case after authorities initially believed no crime had occurred.

item.title: The Trail Jog That Ended in Tragedy: Who Was Rachel Morin?
item.description:
Rachel Morin was a 37 year old Maryland mother of five whose 2023 murder shocked the country after she disappeared while walking on the Ma & Pa Trail in Bel Air, Maryland. She was reported missing after failing to return home from an evening jog, and her body was discovered near the trail the following day.
The investigation quickly became national news as authorities used DNA evidence, surveillance footage, and interstate investigative work to identify a suspect months later. Prosecutors alleged Victor Martinez Hernandez attacked and killed Morin before fleeing the area.
Rachel’s case drew widespread public attention not only because of the violent nature of the crime, but because loved ones described her as a devoted mother trying to rebuild her life. The investigation and trial sparked intense media coverage, online debate, and renewed conversations about safety on public trails and the use of forensic DNA technology in solving major crimes.

media:content.media:title: The Trail Jog That Ended in Tragedy: Who Was Rachel Morin?
media:content.media:description:
Rachel Morin was a 37 year old Maryland mother of five whose 2023 murder shocked the country after she disappeared while walking on the Ma & Pa Trail in Bel Air, Maryland. She was reported missing after failing to return home from an evening jog, and her body was discovered near the trail the following day.
The investigation quickly became national news as authorities used DNA evidence, surveillance footage, and interstate investigative work to identify a suspect months later. Prosecutors alleged Victor Martinez Hernandez attacked and killed Morin before fleeing the area.
Rachel’s case drew widespread public attention not only because of the violent nature of the crime, but because loved ones described her as a devoted mother trying to rebuild her life. The investigation and trial sparked intense media coverage, online debate, and renewed conversations about safety on public trails and the use of forensic DNA technology in solving major crimes.
